questions: how to make a website accessible
screen reader er jonno design kivabe kore
answers: Web accessibility means designing websites and apps that everyone can use, including people with disabilities — visual, auditory, motor, or cognitive impairments.
Accessibility holo shobার jonno product use korar sujog toiri kora. Color contrast thik rakhা, alt text deya, keyboard navigation ensure kora — eishob accessibility er part.
WCAG (Web Content Accessibility Guidelines) provides standards for accessible design. Key principles: Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, and Robust. Aim for at least WCAG 2.1 AA compliance.

To improve accessibility, use semantic HTML, provide alt text for images, ensure sufficient color contrast (4.5:1 ratio), support keyboard navigation, and test with screen readers like NVDA or VoiceOver.
